exemplar.title: To what extent have IKEA's environmental sustainability practices contributed to its long-term profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have IKEA's environmental sustainability practices contributed to its long-term profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies âsustainabilityâ as the key concept and anchors it throughout the analysis
Strong organization-specific discussion, linking IKEA strategies to the concept
Frequent analytical references to renewable-energy investments and circular models
weaknesses: Occasional generic statements without deeper exploration of the three sustainability pillars
Analysis not fully sustained in every paragraph
Introduction could better integrate IKEAâs early history with its sustainability evolution
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work in Band 4: the student demonstrates strong conceptual understanding and clear links to IKEA, but stops short of the seamless, deeply integrated analysis required for Band 5. Occasional generic passages keep the score at 4/5.

strengths: Provides exactly three supporting documents, meeting the 3â5 requirement
All sources are directly relevant to IKEAâs sustainability practices
Documents cited in the text to support analysis
weaknesses: Limited varietyâsources are all IKEA-centric and lack external industry perspectives
Depth of insight is adequate but stops short of providing a broad range of ideas
Could include at least one external report to enrich discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work satisfies Band 3 for Supporting Documents: three relevant and adequately in-depth documents are used, but the narrow focus and limited variety prevent a Band 4 award. Score: 3/4.

strengths: Selects two appropriate tools (stakeholder matrix and financial-accounts analysis)
Applies both tools clearly to the research question
Alignment between tools and question is generally sound
weaknesses: Tool set is adequate but not extensive
Lacks justification for why these tools are most suitable
Diagrams need clearer axis labels and quadrant titles
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments classify this as Band 3: the selection and application are adequate but not consistently effective or well-justified. The student meets Band 3 descriptors but does not fully reach Band 4. Score: 3/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses a substantial range of financial data (2011â2024) and self-constructed graphs
Acknowledges tool limitations and offers some critical evaluation
Draws pertinent links between sustainability initiatives and financial outcomes
weaknesses: Analysis often remains descriptive without rigorous quantification
Key causal claims lack supporting calculations or statistical evidence
Misses opportunities for deeper methods (e.g., regression, ROI metrics)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The data selection meets Band 4, but analysis quality aligns more with Band 3: mostly effective but too descriptive in places. Overall, the mixed depth places this criterion at 3/5.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question in opening sentence
Synthesizes main findings on sustainability and profitability
weaknesses: Some concluding statements are speculative and lack direct data references
Causal links are asserted rather than demonstrated with specific figures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments indicate partial consistency (Band 2) but an explicit, coherent answer to the question (Band 3). The clear answer earns the top band for Conclusions. Score: 3/3.

strengths: Logical, coherent organization throughout (title page, contents, headings, appendices)
Flow within and between sections is clear
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers
Repetitive phrasing in the inquiry question
Could improve navigation by adding a âLimitationsâ subheading
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure fully meets the Band 2 descriptor: an appropriate structure is maintained throughout. Minor navigational issues do not detract from its overall coherence. Score: 2/2.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present (word count, TOC, page numbers, citations, bibliography, appendices)
Consistent use of headings and formatting
weaknesses: Typographical errors in figure captions (e.g., âFigure 4^lâ)
Inconsistent citation style between in-text references and bibliography
Footnote formatting needs correction
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Meets Band 2 for Presentation: all required elements are included and formatted correctly overall. Minor typographical and style inconsistencies keep the score at the top band without deduction. Score: 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical structure with title page, contents list, section headings, numbered appendices and bibliography.  Flow within sections is coherent, fulfilling the criteria for an **appropriate structure throughout** (Band 2).

All three core documents relate directly to sustainability at IKEA and are cited in the text, demonstrating **clear relevance and adequate depth**.  Nevertheless, evidence is drawn mainly from IKEAâs own report and two academic case studies, offering limited variety; the documents do not provide the ârange of ideasâ expected for Band 4.
All required presentation elements for the BM IA (title page with word count, table of contents, page numbers, citations, bibliography and appendices) are present, meeting the top-band requirement.
Exactly three supporting documents are identified in the methodology table, satisfying the requirement of 3â5 sources.  Band 3 is the first band that meets this numerical criterion.

Both tools are applied directly to the research question: the matrix links stakeholdersâ views to profitability; the accounts analysis tracks operating costs, revenue and net income.  Application is generally clear, but some causal claims (e.g., renewable energy single-handedly cutting costs) are asserted rather than proven, so effectiveness is **adequate rather than consistently strong**.

A substantial range of primary financial data (income statements 2011-2024) and self-constructed graphs are used, going beyond the minimum.  Data selection is therefore **sufficient and appropriate**, though not always analysed in depth, placing the work in Band 4.

The student explains how sustainability may influence costs and revenues and notes limitations of each tool, evidencing **some pertinent analysis**.  Yet many links remain descriptive (e.g., âbrand loyalty increases revenueâ) without rigorous quantification or counter-argument, so the analysis is only âmostly effectiveâ in places; Band 3 best captures this mixed quality.

The research question is answered explicitly in the opening sentence of the conclusionââIKEAâs environmental sustainability practices have contributed positively to its long-term profitability.â  This direct response satisfies the Band 3 descriptor.

The student selects two appropriate toolsâthe power-interest stakeholder matrix and financial-accounts analysis.  This is an **adequate but not especially wide** tool set, matching the Band 3 descriptor for selection.

Conclusions broadly restate findings that sustainability improves long-term profitability and mention limitations.  They are **partly grounded** in the earlier data, but the causal chain is not fully demonstrated and some statements remain speculative, so consistency with evidence is partial (Band 2).

Throughout the report the student anchors discussion in IKEAâs specific strategiesâPeople & Planet Positive, wind-turbine and solar investment, circular business modelâand cites concrete income-statement figures from the appendices.  This constitutes **strong organisation-specific analysis**.  Minor sections (e.g., brief definition of sustainability) are more generic, so the integration is âpartialâ rather than âthroughoutâ, aligning with Band 4.

The student identifies âsustainabilityâ as the key concept in the title, introduction and research question and returns to it in all analytic sections (stakeholder matrix, financial-accounts analysis, conclusion).  Clear links are drawn between renewable-energy investment, circular models and profitability, demonstrating **strong conceptual understanding**.  However, the discussion occasionally slips into generic statements (e.g., âsustainability grabs the attention of stakeholdersâ) without analysing the underlying sustainability dimensions (planet, people, profit) in depth, so integration, while frequent, is **not fully sustained across every paragraph**; this fits the descriptor for Band 4 rather than the consistently seamless integration required for Band 5.

annotations.comment: The figure caption âFigure 4^lâ appears to have a typographical error; correct to âFigure 4â for consistency.
The analysis of revenue and net income trends is coherent but would benefit from a brief regression or growth rate calculation to support assertions.
The phrasing repeats âhaveâ twice in the inquiry; streamline to âTo what extent have IKEAâs environmental sustainability practices contributed to long-term profitability?â
The student clearly identifies âSustainabilityâ as the key concept but could briefly acknowledge the three pillars (planet, people, profit) to deepen conceptual framing.
The table of contents lists all sections but omits corresponding page numbers, which hinders navigation. Suggest adding accurate page references.
The inclusion of a clear table of contents with all major headings demonstrates effective structure and organization.
The introduction provides a general definition of sustainability but lacks citation; consider referencing a reputable source (e.g., Brundtland Commission) to support your definition.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: IKEAâs history is well summarized, but the introduction could more directly link early developments to the evolution of its sustainability strategy to strengthen the organizational context.
Only three documents are chosen, all centered on IKEA; to deepen analysis, include at least one external industry report for broader perspective.
The methodology section outlines tools but does not justify why the stakeholder matrix and financial accounts are most suitable; add rationale linking each tool to the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The description of the stakeholder matrix is clear; however, consider adding a brief example to demonstrate its application mechanics.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The power-interest matrix diagram lacks clear axis labels and quadrant titles; include âHigh/Low Powerâ and âHigh/Low Interestâ labels to improve readability.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The narrative notes stakeholder interest in sustainability but stops short of analyzing specific sustainability dimensions (environmental/social/economic); deepen analysis by linking stakeholders to these pillars.
The selection of the power-interest matrix effectively aligns with the research question by mapping stakeholder influence on sustainability initiatives.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The section describes customer loyalty but presents descriptive claims without quantitative evidence; incorporate specific data or survey results to substantiate claims.
The discussion of shareholder attitudes identifies tension but could be enhanced by citing recent IKEA shareholder reports to demonstrate their sustainability priorities.
The student insightfully links IKEAâs circular model to stakeholder interests; consider elaborating how this model quantitatively reduced specific resource costs.
Clearly noting the matrixâs oversimplification and subjectivity demonstrates critical evaluation of your chosen tool.
Figure 2 lacks unit labels on both axes; specify âOperating Costs (EUR million)â and year intervals to enhance clarity.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The claim that 327 wind turbines directly reduced costs should be supported by a brief calculation or reference to operating expenses breakdown.
The caption references âSupporting Document 3â but lacks in-text citation matching the bibliography; use consistent citation style.
The narrative summarizing circular business benefits is descriptive; deepen analysis by quantifying impact on gross margins over specific years.
Footnote marker â1â is displayed as math; revise to standard footnote formatting to align with presentation guidelines.
The limitations discussion reiterates earlier points; deepen by considering additional financial metrics (e.g., ROI, payback period) to assess sustainability impact.
Consider adding a subheading âLimitationsâ to separate methodological critiques and improve readability.
Some statements (e.g., âgreatly influencedâ) are unsubstantiated by earlier quantitative evidence; ensure conclusions directly reference specific data points.
The conclusion explicitly addresses the research question and synthesizes findings, demonstrating a coherent end to the investigation.
